mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive trend in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and economic strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and lifts equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns during periods of rising INDPRO (6-month rolling average up) are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or rank IC <= 0 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5% within a 3-month window, it signals a weakening labor market that erodes consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% over any 3-month period
failure_condition: After trigger activation, the average 5-day forward return is non-negative, or fewer than 60% of triggered instances yield negative returns over a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous tightening through rising federal funds rates and accelerating consumer price inflation indicates an aggressive monetary policy response that raises discount rates and squeezes valuations, producing negative equity returns over a full quarter.
candidate_trigger: both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL 3-month change are positive
failure_condition: When both proxies are rising, the average 60-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the signal generates positive returns in > 40% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: During the late-cycle expansion phase, decelerating real GDP growth signals that aggregate demand is peaking, which precedes a contraction in corporate earnings and a decline in equity prices over the following month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth rate falls below 2% after having been above 3% earlier
failure_condition: When GDP growth decelerates from above 3% to below 2%, the 20-day forward return is positive more than 55% of the time, or rank IC is below 0.05 over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals a contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, which typically leads to weaker economic activity and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST monthly level falls below 1,200 thousand units after being above 1,500
failure_condition: Following trigger activation, the average 60-day forward return is greater than +1% or the probability of a negative return is less than 0.55.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) continues to grow but retail sales (RSAFS) unexpectedly weaken, the divergence reveals a consumer-led demand slowdown that undermines revenue growth and corporate margins, pushing equity prices lower over one month.
failure_condition: When triggered, the average 20-day forward return is positive or the proportion of negative returns is less than 50% over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes real purchasing power and forces the central bank to maintain restrictive policy, which leads to higher discount rates and negative equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return during periods of above-trend CPI (6-month rolling average > 3%) is non-negative on average, or the rank IC is not negative in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle, a decline in the federal funds rate signals accommodative policy that lowers the discount rate on future cash flows and stimulates investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS is negative, the 20-day forward return is below zero in more than 40% of instances over any 3-year period, or the correlation with returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).
